# sysctl settings are defined through files in
# /usr/lib/sysctl.d/, /run/sysctl.d/, and /etc/sysctl.d/.
#
# Vendors settings live in /usr/lib/sysctl.d/.
# To override a whole file, create a new file with the same in

# only specific settings, add a file with a lexically later
# name in /etc/sysctl.d/ and put new settings there.

llm_verdict.message:
The remediation steps have been successfully executed:
1. The kernel parameter `net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter` was set to `1` at runtime via `sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter=1`.
2. Any existing occurrences of the parameter in configuration files under `/etc/sysctl.d`, `/run/sysctl.d`, and `/usr/local/lib/sysctl.d` (excluding symlinks to `/etc/sysctl.conf`) were commented out to prevent duplicates.
3. The line `net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter = 1` was appended to `/etc/sysctl.d/99-sysctl.conf` to make the setting persistent.

Verification shows the parameter is currently set to `1` and the persistence file contains the required line.

The OpenSCAP finding is now remediated.

[END OF REMEDIATION]
